stages.name: Invoice text normalization
stages.risk: Strips pack tokens inconsistently — '125GR*8' vs '2Kg' may normalize to similar cheese tokens

stages.name: Canonical identity extraction
stages.risk: family=cheese for both mozzarella formats; form detection may miss block vs piece

stages.name: Operational family guard
stages.risk: Registry covers fried potato, burger bread, sliced cheese — mozzarella block vs tray not in registry

stages.name: Parent-form hierarchy
stages.risk: Only cheddar family enabled; cheese pack variants explicitly out of scope

stages.name: Matcher scoring + confirm
stages.risk: User/supplier alias confirms wording→ID without pack contract; semantic fallback can bridge distant formats

stages.name: Auto-persist eligibility
stages.risk: Blocks duplicate_normalized_name but not 'same name different pack' if normalized names differ

stages.name: Operational cost persist
stages.risk: Overwrites single catalog price; chains history from any prior row on same ID

canonicalizationRisks.description: Different supplier pack wordings collapse to same normalized_name or match via family token
canonicalizationRisks.vlEvidence: Mozzarella Bocconcino vs Aviludo — same ingredient_id 2a99cecd

canonicalizationRisks.description: Fresh vs preserved (pepino fresco vs conserva) not blocked at match or history chain
History stores €/base-unit but base unit context (g per piece vs g per kg block) is implicit in purchase_quantity only on ingredients row, not on history row
canonicalizationRisks.vlEvidence: Pepino history: 3.748/kg-op vs 0.00177/g-op — math correct per row, chain invalid

canonicalizationRisks.description: confirmed_by_user alias locks supplier shorthand to catalog row without variant disambiguation
canonicalizationRisks.mitigation: ingredient_aliases.confidence exists but no pack_variant_id target

canonicalizationRisks.description: Virtual catalog entries from invoice clustering never persisted — re-match can drift
Pack volume parsed from description affects operational cost but not identity split — 0.20cl vs 33cl would share ID if matched
Supplier intelligence treats all history rows on ingredient_id as comparable supplier quotes
canonicalizationRisks.vlEvidence: buildSupplierIntelligence min90d vs catalogUnit on mixed-format history

canonicalizationRisks.description: All recipes using ingredient_id share one current_price — no recipe-level pack preference

invoiceMatching: Token-family overlap promotes cheese/meat matches without pack check
Supplier shorthand path runs before canonical — can short-circuit to wrong ID
46/51 VL lines unmatched — under-matching avoids some collisions but blocks history sync
aliasPersistence: ingredient_aliases keyed by normalized_alias + optional supplier_name
No recorded purchase_quantity or base_unit at alias confirmation time
priceHistoryLinkage: UNIQUE (invoice_id, ingredient_id) — one history row per invoice per catalog ID
previous_price sourced from latest other-invoice operational price — no format gate

whatWorksToday: Auto-persist blocks obvious operational_family_conflict (e.g. fried potato vs potato bread)
duplicate_normalized_name prevents exact duplicate catalog inserts
Delta math on stored operational values is correct
Sub-recipe graph has clean XOR ingredient_id / sub_recipe_id
Alias table has confidence + confirmed_by_user columns (underutilized for pack disambiguation)
